channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.rockpapershotgun.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.rockpapershotgun.com

item.title: Marathon's next experimental mode goes all in on sponsored kits, which sounds great for scaredy-cats like me
item.content:encoded:
There's another experiment on the way to Marathon. Last month, Bungie started testing a duos mode in the extraction shooter. According to an update from Joe Ziegler, that mode will be making its way into the game as a full feature in season two, with Bungie "diligently working on delivering that to all of you who are eagerly awaiting its return." But with this update comes the next test: a new mode that forces you into using sponsored kits.

Starting from tomorrow, April 15th at 6pm BST/1pm ET/ 10am PT, and ending on April 29th at the same time, a Dire Marsh - Sponsored Queue option will be available to dive into. "This is a new experimental queue that is focused on learning a bit more about our early gear ecosystem," Ziegler explained in a Steam blog post. Rather than loading into a game with any kit you've accrued, here you'll start with a free sponsored kit, and will have to make do with whatever you find in Dire Marsh.

To queue up for it you'll have to first make sure you have a white tier free sponsored kit equipped, so some menu fiddling will be required. You'll also be able to enter as a premade duo (crew fill off), premade trio, or crew fill into a trio. In terms of what Bungie will be keeping an eye on, they want to understand "how the game plays out with low fixed set gear conditions and caps," "how the zero to hero play works in low starting set gear conditions and caps," and any tech issues that come up.

Considering the explicit challenge that's put forth by Marathon and its genre, a consequence free mode like this sounds like it could be a strong way to ease new people into it. Seems like a great opportunity to test out other shells too, almost like a self-imposed tutorial. I continue to hold a curiosity about Marathon because the vibe is tuned to my exact brainwave, but the impermanency of it all feels a tad offputting. I just don't want to lose my stuff! Perhaps this will be my in.

Marathon's mid-season update rolls out today too, bringing with it weapon adjustments, commendations, and more. You can read about all the additions in the patch notes.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/marathons-next-experimental-mode-goes-all-in-on-sponsored-kits-which-sounds-great-for-scaredy-cats-like-me
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Phew, American Truck Simulator's latest beta update will tow me back to the road when I inevitably roll it trying to take a turn at 80 mph
item.content:encoded:
The same handy feature's also on its way to Euro Truck Sim 2

I'm happily trucking down the highway in American Truck Simulator. The radio's on, it's a while to my next mandatory break, and there's not too much traffic. Things are going we-OH NO, OH BALLS, THAT'S MY TURN! I'M JERKING THE WHEEL VIOLENTLY, I'M TURNING, I'VE FORGOTTEN TO BR. Right, now my truck needs to be turned the right way up. Usually, I'd need to be dragged all the way to the nearest service station, but thanks to a new feature SCS Software are starting to roll out to their truck sims, I can now be neatly plopped back onto the road.

Matter-of-factly dubbed tow to road, this new option in the established services and adjustments menu all of us rush to in ATS or its European sibling Euro Truck Simulator 2 whenever we get properly stuck is arriving as part of both games' versions of update 1.59. Since an open beta for that update's just gone live on Steam for American Truck Sim, you can give tow to road a go right now if you do your trucking stateside.

SCS Software explained in the open beta notes that they've opted to add tow to road in now in order to give folks who've had minor accidents a chance to avoid "unnecessary delays" in getting going again, especially if they're trying to keep up with a multiplayer convoy. The standard tow to service option's still there in its usual place, but is now the one to go to only if you've done significant enough damage to your truck that you feel you need to get to to a mechanic right away. If you're good to keep on motoring after a quick dislodging or want to bother fixing any scrapes further down the line, tow to road's your new number one choice.

With a small fee paid to the towing service, selecting it will see you whisked off to whatever the game dubs your "last safe location" on the road. It's worth noting that if you stray too far from the road, the feature might not pick up a "last safe location", this being a failsafe SCS have built in seemingly to stop people from trying to use tow to road for wanton warping around. You'll also want to make sure your trailer stays hitched up, otherwise tow to road'll just whisk the head of your lorry off to the safe point, abandoning your cargo.

As an impatient idiot who regularly flaunts ATS's speed limits and red signals out of sheer laziness, as well as being a sucker for thinking things like 'I don't need an extra point to make this turn, I'll just-and I'm stuck on a bush that was more solid than I thought', this is an addition I've been waiting for ever since I was handed the keys to my first rig. Will it save me from my own stupidity? Inevitably, at least in cases when I've not hit enough that my dashboard warning lights are lit up like a Christmas tree.

If you fancy giving ATS's 1.59 update beta a go, you can opt into it by heading to the betas section of the properties menu which is accessible via right-clicking on the game in your Steam library. The beta also introduces a number of other handy features and tweaks, such as a widget that makes checking how damaged your truck is a bit more convenient, a skill screen redesign, and adjustments to traffic audio.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/phew-american-truck-simulators-latest-beta-update-will-tow-me-back-to-the-road-when-i-inevitably-roll-it-trying-to-take-a-turn-at-80-mph
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: STALKER 2’s latest update adds an easily missed new questline, while dispersing accidental mutant crowds
item.content:encoded:
Sealed Truth update nixes some glitches and teases the Cost of Hope expansion

Get in here, stalker. STALKER 2: Heart of Chornobyl just got its meatiest patch in months, the Sealed Truth update, which has added a spooky, story-based new questline along with various bug fixes.

The new quests, which take you deep into the monster-infested bowels of the X-18 laboratory (previously visited in STALKER: Shadow of Chernobyl), seemingly play up HoC’s horror side, and word is it’ll loosely tie into the plot of the incoming Cost of Hope expansion. Which sounds good, although you might need to fire up an older save to kick it off, as it’s seemingly only available for a brief period early in the game.

Well, I say "brief". Depending on how far you stalk from the main mission flow, that window could last hours, though it’s still a finite opportunity. Basically, you can start the Sealed Truth storyline by speaking to Diode, the resident techie inside stalker hub/literal trash heap Garbage, but only between (minor spoilers for a 17-month-old shooter) meeting Scar at his sawmill camp and visiting SIRCAA headquarters with the retrieved Scanner. Before or after these respective points, Diode’s dialogue option to begin the questline isn’t available, even if he’s alive and uneaten. Harsh!

Annoyingly, this detail isn’t elaborated in GSC’s patch notes; I’ve double-checked it by jumping through my own saves, but did only find out the timing through a video by reliably in-the-know STALKERtuber Vatislavovych. I suppose it’s possibly that the questline itself requires setting in the pre-SIRCAA shenanigans period for its plot to make sense, though it’s a shame that Sealed Truth doesn’t have as flexible a starting point as STALKER 2’s previous free narrative-heavy update, Stories Untold.

At least the new bug fixes can be more universally enjoyed. Addressed issues include dangerous anomalies catching/burning/crushing you in a wider than intended radius, a few instances of excessively clippy hand animations, and one particularly lethal techno-whoopsie where big groups of mutants would spawn together. Not such a big deal if it’s just an unusually large pack of radioactive dogs, but less than ideal when it’s a dozen thirsty Bloodsuckers.

The Sealed Truth update is out now, for free; Cost of Hope is coming sometime this Summer, almost definitely for Not Free.



https://www.rockpapershotgun.com/stalker-2s-latest-update-adds-an-easily-missed-new-questline-while-dispersing-accidental-mutant-crowds
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
